title: Human Health and Disease
subtitle: Immunity, common diseases, AIDS, cancer, drugs, alcohol and adolescent health - a premium visual clinical revision guide.

heroModel.title: Immune defense model
heroModel.subtitle: Drag to rotate - scroll to zoom

roadmapTitle: Health is maintained by prevention, immunity and responsible choices.
roadmapDescription: Learn disease examples as cause -> transmission -> symptoms -> prevention, then connect immunity, cancer and substance abuse.
flowchart:
flowchart LR
  A[Health] --> B[Pathogens]
  B --> C[Common diseases]
  C --> D[Immunity]
  D --> E[Vaccination]
  C --> F[AIDS]
  C --> G[Cancer]
  A --> H[Drugs and alcohol]
  H --> I[Prevention and support]

roadmap.title: Meaning of health
roadmap.summary: Physical, mental and social well-being, not just absence of disease.

roadmap.title: Common diseases
roadmap.summary: Pathogens cause infectious diseases with specific transmission routes.

roadmap.title: Innate immunity
roadmap.summary: Non-specific barriers and cells act quickly.

roadmap.title: AIDS
roadmap.summary: HIV attacks helper T cells and weakens immunity.

roadmap.title: Prevention
roadmap.summary: Hygiene, vaccination, diagnosis, counselling and support.

conceptTitle: Clinical facts organised for fast recall.
conceptDescription: The chapter becomes easy when every disease is remembered by pathogen, spread, symptoms and prevention.

sections.title: Health and disease basics
sections.description: Health includes physical, mental and social well-being.
points: Disease can be infectious or non-infectious.
Pathogens include viruses, bacteria, protozoans, fungi and helminths.
Lifestyle and environment influence health.
Prevention is often easier than treatment.

sections.title: Common infectious diseases
sections.description: NCERT examples include typhoid, pneumonia, common cold, malaria, amoebiasis, ascariasis and ringworm.
points: Typhoid is caused by Salmonella typhi.
Pneumonia can be caused by Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae.
Malaria is caused by Plasmodium and spread by female Anopheles mosquito.
Amoebiasis is caused by Entamoeba histolytica.

sections.title: Innate immunity
sections.description: Innate immunity is non-specific defense present from birth.
points: Physical barriers include skin and mucous lining.
Physiological barriers include acid, saliva and tears.
Cellular barriers include phagocytes and NK cells.
Cytokine barriers include interferons.
sections.neet: Interferons protect non-infected cells from viral infection.

sections.title: Acquired immunity and vaccination
sections.description: Acquired immunity is specific and has memory.
points: B cells produce antibodies.
T cells support cellular immunity.
Primary response is slow; secondary response is faster and stronger.
Vaccines create immunological memory.
sections.neet: Memory cells make secondary response faster.

sections.title: HIV and AIDS
sections.description: HIV destroys helper T lymphocytes and causes immunodeficiency.
points: HIV spreads through sexual contact, infected blood, shared needles and mother to child.
HIV infects macrophages and T helper cells.
AIDS causes vulnerability to opportunistic infections.
ELISA can help detect HIV infection.
sections.neet: HIV does not spread through casual contact.

sections.title: Cancer biology
sections.description: Cancer involves uncontrolled cell division and spread.
points: Proto-oncogenes can become oncogenes.
Tumours may be benign or malignant.
Metastasis is spread of cancer cells to distant sites.
Radiation, chemicals and viruses can be carcinogenic.
sections.neet: Metastasis is a key feature of malignant tumours.

sections.title: Drugs, alcohol and dependence
sections.description: Substance abuse can lead to tolerance, dependence and withdrawal symptoms.
points: Opioids bind opioid receptors.
Cannabinoids affect brain and cardiovascular system.
Cocaine interferes with dopamine transport.
Adolescents are especially vulnerable to peer pressure.
sections.neet: Addiction is psychological attachment; dependence causes withdrawal symptoms.

sections.title: Prevention and support
sections.description: Disease prevention combines hygiene, vaccination, early diagnosis and counselling.
points: Safe water and sanitation prevent many infections.
Vector control reduces mosquito-borne disease.
Vaccination prevents many serious infections.
Counselling and family support help substance-abuse recovery.
sections.neet: Prevention and early detection are repeatedly tested themes.

modelDescription: Immunity, pathogens, diagnosis and prevention become visual anchors.

models.title: Cancer warning model
models.description: Uncontrolled cell cluster as risk anchor.

models.title: Population prevention
models.description: Disease spread and prevention at population level.

highYield: Health includes physical, mental and social well-being.
Typhoid is caused by Salmonella typhi.
Acquired immunity is specific and has memory.
Addiction and dependence are not the same.

mistakes.title: HIV transmission myth
mistakes.body: HIV does not spread by touch, sharing food or casual contact.

mistakes.title: Primary vs secondary response
mistakes.body: Secondary immune response is faster and stronger due to memory cells.

mistakes.title: Benign vs malignant
mistakes.body: Malignant tumours invade and metastasise; benign tumours usually remain localised.

quiz.question: Interferons are part of:
quiz.explain: Interferons are cytokines released during viral infection.

quiz.question: HIV primarily reduces:
quiz.explain: HIV infects and destroys helper T lymphocytes.

quiz.question: Metastasis means:
quiz.explain: Malignant cancer cells can spread to distant sites.

quiz.question: ELISA is associated with diagnosis of:
quiz.explain: ELISA is used in HIV detection.

quiz.question: Secondary immune response is faster due to:
Dependence on drugs often causes:
quiz.explain: Dependence can produce withdrawal symptoms when drug is stopped.
